response_raw:
{
"mode": "human",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "As a seasoned journalist, I have seen the damage that fake news can cause. It's essential to be vigilant and critical when consuming information online. Here are some tips to help you manage and deal with fake content on social media: 1) Verify the source: Before sharing any information, make sure it comes from a reputable source. Check the URL and see if the article has been verified by fact-checking organizations. 2) Be cautious of sensational headlines: If a headline seems too good (or bad) to be true, it probably is. Be wary of headlines that try to elicit an emotional response. 3) Look for corroboration: See if other reputable sources are reporting the same information. If not, it's likely a fake news story. 4) Use fact-checking websites: Websites like Snopes, FactCheck.org, and PolitiFact can help you verify the accuracy of information. 5) Be aware of confirmation bias: It's easy to fall prey to confirmation bias, where we only seek out information that confirms our existing beliefs. Make a conscious effort to seek out diverse viewpoints and consider different perspectives. 6) Use social media platforms' built-in fact-checking tools: Many social media platforms have implemented fact-checking measures, such as flagging suspicious content or providing alternative sources. Take advantage of these features to help you identify and avoid fake content. By following these tips, you can become a more discerning consumer of information on social media and help to combat the spread of fake news.",
"reasoning_summary": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, verify the source, be cautious of sensational headlines, look for corroboration, use fact-checking websites, be aware of confirmation bias, and use social media platforms' built-in fact-checking tools.",
"tone": "informative",

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: As a seasoned journalist, I have seen the damage that fake news can cause. It's essential to be vigilant and critical when consuming information online. Here are some tips to help you manage and deal with fake content on social media: 1) Verify the source: Before sharing any information, make sure it comes from a reputable source. Check the URL and see if the article has been verified by fact-checking organizations. 2) Be cautious of sensational headlines: If a headline seems too good (or bad) to be true, it probably is. Be wary of headlines that try to elicit an emotional response. 3) Look for corroboration: See if other reputable sources are reporting the same information. If not, it's likely a fake news story. 4) Use fact-checking websites: Websites like Snopes, FactCheck.org, and PolitiFact can help you verify the accuracy of information. 5) Be aware of confirmation bias: It's easy to fall prey to confirmation bias, where we only seek out information that confirms our existing beliefs. Make a conscious effort to seek out diverse viewpoints and consider different perspectives. 6) Use social media platforms' built-in fact-checking tools: Many social media platforms have implemented fact-checking measures, such as flagging suspicious content or providing alternative sources. Take advantage of these features to help you identify and avoid fake content. By following these tips, you can become a more discerning consumer of information on social media and help to combat the spread of fake news.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, verify the source, be cautious of sensational headlines, look for corroboration, use fact-checking websites, be aware of confirmation bias, and use social media platforms' built-in fact-checking tools.
